content: Is there a plan to bring aarch64 into supported status?
I think we have aarch64 support, it's just that it's not in v10
I know. I'm wondering what it takes to put it there. Perhaps a follow-up patch after the series is merged is good enough?
We will likely need an Acked-by of something from ARM64 maintainer though.

The idea would be to get someone from arm64 involved -- the non-x86 arches we have work for the config we run in the CI, but they should be properly split into their Makefile + target spec script and customized as needed according to the config like it is done for x86, which ideally an expert on each arch would do (and agree to maintain it).
I can do that for aarch64, though I'm not an expert ;-)
Once the basic support is in mainline, I will prepare some patchset to add aarch64 support.

Miguel Ojeda said:

The idea would be to get someone from arm64 involved -- the non-x86 arches we have work for the config we run in the CI, but they should be properly split into their Makefile + target spec script and customized as needed according to the config like it is done for x86, which ideally an expert on each arch would do (and agree to maintain it).

There is no x86 maintainer who agrees to maintain the Rust code, right?
No, we cheat on that ;-) Peter has asked a few questions about Rust compiler options, and we hope he can help us.
Yeah, that was the exception and is intended to be used as the example for other arches :)

Peter wanted to see support for everything on day 1, including mitigations, which is a bit too ambitious... In the end,rustc is yet another compiler that will also need time to get features like GCC and Clang do when something new arrives.

I gave him an example of porting a mitigation that just arrived at the time to LLVM into rustc's LLVM and used it from Rust [1]. But yeah, we will need time to get all the pieces in the right places.

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/CANiq72k_dtr2=KX-WheUiM2v2370imAdWFawJR+8P41L7HjwpA@mail.gmail.com/
This is not specific to Rust though -- Clang built Linux will also have the same issues.
FWIW, I asked Ard Biesheuvel to take a look at the existing aarch64 porting work, so I'm hoping he'll add some reviews/acks when the time comes. :)
